currency_note: All dollar figures are indicative AUD screening-model outputs, not quoted transaction values or independently verified NPVs.

claims.statement: Indicative annual electricity demand for the Fountain Head operation is approximately 30 GWh per year.
claims.limitations: The draft EIS text (s3.8.1.1) states 'approximately 30 MWh/yr', an apparent unit inconsistency. The ~30 GWh/yr figure is derived by cross-checking the stated 3.9 MW peak, 7,500 kL/yr diesel fuel use and Table 3.19 energy produced (107,853 GJ/yr = 29.96 GWh/yr). Owner-confirmed load data is required before design work.

claims.statement: Indicative site peak electrical load is approximately 3.9 MW, from a proposed diesel power station of three 1.5 MW units.
claims.limitations: Stated in the draft EIS as the proposed power-station configuration; actual operating load profile is not public.

claims.statement: The proposed diesel power station is expected to consume approximately 7,500 kL of diesel per year during operations.
A solar PV installation of approximately 4.6 MWp has been publicly disclosed as a starting point for the site's hybrid-energy configuration.
claims.limitations: Figure as cited in Heliovulcan's public report from PNX Metals / Sunrise public reporting; the primary disclosure URL is not yet captured in this registry (see evidence EVID-FH-PNX-01, status unresolved).

claims.statement: A battery energy storage system of approximately 2.3 MW / 2.2 MWh has been publicly disclosed as a starting point for the site's hybrid-energy configuration.
claims.limitations: Figure as cited in Heliovulcan's public report from PNX Metals / Sunrise public reporting; the primary disclosure URL is not yet captured in this registry (see evidence EVID-FH-PNX-01, status unresolved).

claims.statement: The screening model assumes a specific solar yield of 1,850 kWh/kWp/yr for the Pine Creek region.
claims.limitations: Analyst assumption informed by Global Solar Atlas regional data; not a bankable yield assessment (no P50/P90 study, no on-site measurement).

claims.statement: Of the four screened configurations (S1 short-life, S3 expanded hub, S4 60% renewable target, S5 100% renewable upper bound), S4 is the scenario selected for further hourly and commercial validation.
claims.limitations: A screening selection, not a design recommendation or investment advice. S4 requires 8,760-hour dispatch, reliability, curtailment and battery-degradation validation.

claims.statement: Modelled total installed cost (TIC before IDC) for scenario S4 is approximately A$31.3M.
claims.limitations: Screening-level EPC benchmark costing; can move materially before a binding EPC quote.

claims.statement: Modelled SPV after-tax CFADS (base case) for scenario S4 is approximately A$5.7M per year.
claims.limitations: Model output under unverified timing, pricing, financing and contract assumptions.

claims.statement: Indicative debt capacity for scenario S4 is approximately A$20.5M when debt is resized to a simplified 1.30x minimum-DSCR constraint.
claims.limitations: The 1.30x DSCR is a debt-sizing constraint used in the model, not evidence that any lender has approved any scenario.

claims.statement: The modelled residual / buy-out payment for scenario S4 is approximately A$4.5M.
claims.limitations: Residual timing, discount rate, inflation basis, tax treatment and ownership transfer require confirmation; not a quoted transaction value.

claims.statement: The indicative owner outcome for scenario S4 after the modelled buy-out is approximately positive A$4.6M, the only positive owner outcome among the four screened scenarios.
claims.limitations: Model output over an assumed comparison period; must be validated against owner-confirmed mine plans, load data and real contract / buy-out terms.

claims.statement: The short-life base case (S1) produces a modelled residual of approximately A$27.3M and a negative indicative owner outcome of approximately -A$20.1M.
claims.limitations: Illustrates why short mine life / contract tenor undermines third-party SPV capital recovery; requires confirmation of timing, discounting, ownership and contract terms.

claims.statement: The PV and BESS/PCS areas identified in the siting screen are preliminary desktop search areas, not equipment footprints, approved buildable land or owner-selected sites.
claims.limitations: Candidate areas require owner data, field verification, specialist assessment and engineering validation.
